etymology.description: 橙(橙) (chéng, "orange") + 汁(汁) (zhī, "juice") forms the term 橙汁 (chéngzhī, "orange juice"), referring to the liquid extracted from oranges.
etymology.interpretation: 橙(橙) (chéng, "orange") refers to the fruit known for its vibrant color and citrus flavor. When combined with 汁(汁) (zhī, "juice"), which denotes the liquid content obtained by pressing or squeezing fruits, it specifies the juice derived from oranges. Thus, 橙汁 captures the notion of the liquid essence of the orange fruit, commonly used as a refreshing drink.

examples.chinese: 我早餐喝了一杯橙汁(我早餐喝了一杯橙汁)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zǎocān hēle yī bēi chéngzhī.
examples.english: I had a glass of orange juice for breakfast.

examples.chinese: 橙汁(橙汁)是维生素C的重要来源(是維生素C的重要來源)。
examples.pinyin: Chéngzhī shì wéishēngsù C de zhòngyào láiyuán.
examples.english: Orange juice is an important source of vitamin C.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢在午餐时喝橙汁(她喜歡在午餐時喝橙汁)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài wǔcān shí hē chéngzhī.
examples.english: She likes to drink orange juice during lunch.
